g.data-narration: Welcome to Flip-Flops - the fundamental memory elements that give digital circuits the power to remember past states.
Combinational circuits output depends only on inputs. To build memory, we need circuits that remember past events.
A flip-flop is a bistable multivibrator with two stable states that stores one bit of binary information indefinitely.
g.text: Symbol: Q = current state, Q' = complement

g.data-narration: Level triggering activates during entire clock phase, while edge triggering only activates at the precise moment of transition.
The SR Flip-Flop is the simplest type with Set and Reset inputs. Note the forbidden state when both inputs are HIGH.
g.text: Most basic flip-flop type with two inputs: S (Set) and R (Reset)

g.data-narration: The JK Flip-Flop improves on SR by eliminating the forbidden state. When both inputs are HIGH, it toggles the output.
g.text: Refines SR by eliminating invalid state. J=Set, K=Reset. When J=K=1, output TOGGLES!

g.data-narration: The D Flip-Flop is the most practical type. It simply transfers the D input value to output Q at the clock edge.
g.text: Most commonly used in modern design. Q(t+1) = D. Simple and no invalid states!

g.data-narration: The T Flip-Flop has a single toggle input. When T=1, output complements itself. Perfect for counters and frequency dividers.
g.text: Single input T. When T=1, output toggles. Essential for counters and frequency division.

g.data-narration: Watch how a positive-edge-triggered D Flip-Flop samples input only at rising edges and holds the value between edges.
g.text: Positive-edge-triggered D Flip-Flop samples D only at rising edge (↑) and holds value until next edge.

g.data-narration: At each rising edge, the D value is sampled. Q changes to match D at that instant and holds until next rising edge.
Comparing flip-flops: SR is basic but has forbidden state. JK fixes this with toggle. D is most practical. T is ideal for counters.
Flip-flops are everywhere: computer memory cells, CPU registers, digital clocks, and communication buffers.
g.text: Every digital device contains millions of flip-flops!

g.data-narration: Flip-flops are built from basic gates. A simple SR latch uses NOR gates with cross-coupled feedback for memory.
g.text: Flip-flops are constructed from basic logic gates with cross-coupled feedback loops that create memory.

g.data-narration: Each flip-flop has a characteristic equation defining its next state. These equations are essential for circuit design and analysis.
g.text: These equations define Q(t+1) in terms of inputs and current state Q(t). Essential for sequential circuit design.

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: Flip-flops are 1-bit memory elements synchronized by clock, edge-triggered for precision, with four main types.
